IRGC identified as a threat actor engaging in active mine-emplacement operations, a confirmed hostile action beyond the prior report's general closure of the Strait.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Activate the organisation's armed-conflict contingency protocol for the
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Chief Risk Officer must formally activate the organisation's armed-conflict contingency protocol no later than COB 27 May 2026, logging the 25 May 2026 CENTCOM self-defense strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and mine-emplacement boats in the Strait of Hormuz as the qualifying trigger event that invalidates any planning assumption premised on ceasefire stability in the corridor. The activation record must explicitly retire the prior baseline assumption that the Strait remained nominally open under a negotiated pause, replacing it with an assumption of active kinetic conflict in the waterway, and must reassign all Hormuz-dependent route plans and contract performance schedules to the armed-conflict contingency track. Evidence of completion is a dated activation memo countersigned by the CRO and General Counsel, with the trigger-event log entry timestamped to CENTCOM's 25 May 2026 announcement, filed in the enterprise risk management system within 24 hours of this control's issuance.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic armed-conflict protocol does not specify which event invalidates the ceasefire-stability assumption; this adaptation names the precise CENTCOM strike announcement of 25 May 2026 as that trigger, preventing ambiguity over whether a prior ceasefire or the Pakistan-brokered pause still justifies legacy planning baselines.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Update the geopolitical scenario register to include a
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Geopolitical Risk team must update the enterprise scenario register by 30 May 2026 to add a 'ceasefire collapse' branch originating from the 25 May 2026 CENTCOM strikes, with probability weightings and consequence assessments distinct from the existing 'prolonged blockade' scenario already reflecting the Strait's closure since 28 February 2026. This new branch must define at least three red-line indicators tied specifically to the Qatar-mediated MOU negotiation: (i) public breakdown of talks over Iran's nuclear enrichment language, (ii) IRGC resumption of mine-laying operations in the Strait, and (iii) Iran's Foreign Minister issuing a formal withdrawal from the MOU framework — each indicator triggering a pre-defined escalation response in the scenario register. The completed register entry, including indicator definitions and escalation responses, must be reviewed and signed off by the Head of Geopolitical Risk and submitted to the Risk Committee before the next scheduled meeting or within five business days, whichever is sooner.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic scenario register update does not distinguish between a new 'ceasefire collapse' branch and the existing prolonged-blockade scenario already in effect since February 2026; this adaptation creates a structurally separate branch with red-line indicators specific to the Qatar MOU negotiation dynamics and Iran's nuclear enrichment language disputes.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Engage government-affairs and legal counsel within 72h to
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Head of Government Affairs must convene a working session with external OFAC counsel within 72 hours of 25 May 2026 to assess whether the US Government's 'self-defense strikes' characterisation of the CENTCOM action alters OFAC enforcement posture toward counterparties the organisation transacts with under existing Iran General Licenses, with specific focus on whether the strikes signal a shift toward stricter secondary-sanctions enforcement against entities that have paid or may pay transit fees to Iran's Persian Gulf Strait Authority. The assessment must cover all active Iran General Licenses held by the organisation, identify any counterparty that has transited the Strait since 5 May 2026 when the Persian Gulf Strait Authority was established, and produce a written risk opinion distinguishing transactions that remain within license scope from those requiring immediate suspension or amendment. The completed legal opinion, including a list of flagged counterparties and recommended transaction actions, must be delivered to the Chief Compliance Officer and General Counsel no later than 28 May 2026.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic sanctions-assessment requirement does not address the specific enforcement-posture question raised by the US framing of strikes as 'self-defense' alongside its concurrent warning to shipping companies about sanctions for paying the newly established Persian Gulf Strait Authority; this adaptation directs counsel to assess that precise framing-and-enforcement linkage rather than conducting a routine license review.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Conduct a crisis-communication review of all public statements
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Head of Corporate Communications must initiate an immediate audit of all external-facing materials — including press releases, investor presentations, website content, and previously issued regulatory filings — that reference 'ceasefire stability,' 'open Strait of Hormuz,' or 'normalising transit conditions' in the context of the Persian Gulf corridor, completing the audit no later than 27 May 2026 close of business. Any identified materials must be revised or supplemented with language that accurately reflects the contested ceasefire status as of 25–26 May 2026 — specifically acknowledging that CENTCOM conducted strikes on Iranian missile sites and mine-laying boats on 25 May 2026 and that Iran's Foreign Minister has publicly condemned these as a ceasefire violation — before any further public dissemination. A consolidated list of all reviewed materials, the revisions made, and the sign-off obtained from Legal must be filed with the Chief Communications Officer and General Counsel as the evidence of completion.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic crisis-communication review does not identify the specific factual claim — ceasefire stability — that has been rendered misleading by the simultaneous occurrence of CENTCOM strikes and Iran's condemnation on 25–26 May 2026; this adaptation targets that precise language category and anchors the required correction to the specific public facts now on record.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Brief the board within five business days on
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Chief Strategy Officer must deliver a board briefing within five business days of 25 May 2026 quantifying the organisation's direct and indirect exposure to the two unresolved sticking points in the Qatar-mediated MOU negotiations — Iran's nuclear enrichment programme and the timing of phased sanctions relief — with scenario analysis covering at least three outcomes: (i) MOU reached with full sanctions relief within 30 days; (ii) talks collapse over enrichment language, extending the Strait closure beyond the 28 February 2026 baseline; and (iii) partial MOU reached with staged relief, leaving IRGC-related sanctions on Persian Gulf Strait Authority transit fees unresolved. For each scenario, the briefing must quantify the organisation's direct Hormuz transit dependency and indirect exposure through energy-input costs, supply-chain counterparty disruption, and war-risk insurance gaps, expressed in dollar-denominated P&L impact ranges. The board pack, including the scenario analysis and exposure quantification, must be distributed to board members no later than 30 May 2026 with a standing agenda item reserved for questions.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic board briefing requirement does not specify that the two named unresolved sticking points — nuclear enrichment language and sanctions-relief timing — each produce structurally different exposure profiles for the organisation; this adaptation requires scenario-differentiated quantification rather than a single aggregate exposure statement.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Verify that third-party maritime operators and logistics counterparties
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Chief Compliance Officer must direct the procurement and logistics compliance team to survey all third-party maritime operators, freight forwarders, and logistics counterparties serving the Persian Gulf corridor by 29 May 2026, requiring each to certify in writing whether they have paid any fee, tariff, or transit charge to Iran's Persian Gulf Strait Authority — established on 5 May 2026 — for any vessel passage since that date. Any counterparty that confirms or cannot rule out such payment must be placed on a transaction hold pending OFAC counsel review, given the US warning that paying Iran for Strait transit exposes companies to secondary sanctions under the IRGC-related sanctions architecture. The survey instrument, all counterparty certifications received, and a log of counterparties placed on transaction hold must be delivered to the General Counsel and Chief Compliance Officer as evidence of completion, with the full survey completed no later than 29 May 2026.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic third-party verification requirement does not anchor the compliance obligation to the specific institutional mechanism — Iran's Persian Gulf Strait Authority established 5 May 2026 — or to the US secondary-sanctions warning that specifically targets payment of transit fees to that Authority; this adaptation makes the survey obligation traceable to those named facts.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Test the organisation's supply-chain business continuity plan against
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Head of Supply Chain and the Head of Energy Procurement must jointly conduct a business continuity stress test by 6 June 2026 that models a 90-day full Hormuz closure extending the blockade already in effect since 28 February 2026, quantifying for each affected energy input the incremental cost of: (i) rerouting bulk carriers via the Cape of Good Hope, including additional voyage days, fuel costs, and vessel availability constraints; and (ii) activating the Abu Dhabi Crude Oil Pipeline bypass where applicable, including throughput capacity limits and tariff costs. The stress test must produce a line-item cost impact table per input category, identify any supply volume shortfall that cannot be covered by either route under the 90-day scenario, and specify the contractual mechanisms or spot-market alternatives available to cover those gaps. The completed stress-test report must be reviewed by the CFO and submitted to the Risk Committee no later than 6 June 2026, with the methodology and assumptions documented in sufficient detail for independent validation.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic BCP test requirement does not distinguish between the Cape of Good Hope rerouting option and the Abu Dhabi Crude Oil Pipeline bypass as structurally different mitigation levers with different throughput constraints; this adaptation requires both to be quantified and compared against actual identified supply gaps rather than treated as interchangeable alternatives.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Invoke force majeure review procedures for all logistics
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The General Counsel must initiate a force majeure clause review for every logistics contract, offtake agreement, and freight arrangement whose performance depends on Strait of Hormuz transit, using 25 May 2026 as the qualifying trigger date for the CENTCOM self-defense strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and mine-laying boats, and must complete a preliminary triage of all such contracts within five business days. For each contract identified, Legal must determine whether the force majeure clause is triggered by the 25 May event independent of or in addition to the existing Strait closure since 28 February 2026, assess whether any notice obligation has already been missed relative to the February closure trigger, and produce a recommended action — invoke, preserve optionality, or waive — with a rationale for each. The triage output must be presented to the CFO and Chief Operating Officer by 30 May 2026, with priority notices dispatched to counterparties on contracts where Legal recommends immediate invocation.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic force majeure review does not address the legally material question of whether the 25 May 2026 CENTCOM strike constitutes a separate qualifying event from the original 28 February 2026 Strait closure — a distinction that determines whether notice periods and invocation windows have reset or are already expired.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Establish a daily operational watch cadence monitoring CENTCOM
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Chief Operating Officer must establish a daily operational watch cadence effective immediately, staffed by at least one designated analyst monitoring CENTCOM public affairs releases, IRGC official statements, and Qatar MOU negotiation communiqués every 24 hours for the duration of the Qatar-mediated negotiations toward a Hormuz MOU. The watch cadence must include a pre-defined 48-hour escalation trigger: if CENTCOM or credible maritime-incident reporting identifies resumed mine-laying operations by Iranian boats in the Strait of Hormuz — the same activity targeted in the 25 May 2026 strikes — or any new anti-ship missile launch from Iranian sites within 48 hours of any stated ceasefire extension, the analyst must immediately escalate to the COO, CRO, and General Counsel and convene an emergency operations call within four hours. The watch cadence must be documented in a standing operating procedure, with daily watch logs retained as evidence of completion, and must remain active until a final MOU is signed or negotiations formally collapse.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic operational watch requirement does not define the specific behavioural indicators — resumed mine-laying and anti-ship missile activity — that mirror the exact threat acts targeted in the 25 May 2026 strikes, nor does it tie the escalation trigger to ceasefire-extension announcements as the relevant reset event; this adaptation closes both gaps.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Quantify the P&L sensitivity to a $20/barrel Brent
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The CFO must direct the FP&A team to produce a P&L sensitivity analysis by 2 June 2026 that quantifies the organisation's earnings exposure to a $20-per-barrel Brent crude spike scenario driven by a continued or worsening Hormuz closure following the 25 May 2026 CENTCOM strikes, expressed as a range of pre-tax income impact across the organisation's energy-cost and commodity-exposed business lines. The analysis must use the pre-escalation price context — US gas prices at approximately $4.46 per gallon before the 25 May event — as the baseline, model the incremental margin compression from each $5/barrel increment up to $20/barrel above that baseline, and identify any natural hedges or existing derivatives positions that offset part of the exposure. The sensitivity table and hedging assessment must be incorporated into the next quarterly financial report as a named disclosure item referencing the Strait of Hormuz closure and the 25 May 2026 escalation event, with the draft disclosure language reviewed by Legal before filing.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic price-sensitivity requirement does not establish the specific baseline — the pre-25 May 2026 price environment with US gas at $4.46/gallon — against which the incremental escalation impact must be measured; this adaptation anchors the sensitivity analysis to that named baseline so the disclosure captures the marginal effect of the specific escalation rather than total energy-cost exposure.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Review war-risk insurance coverage adequacy for all assets
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Chief Financial Officer must commission a war-risk insurance adequacy review for all organisation-owned or counterparty-operated assets with Persian Gulf exposure, to be completed by 30 May 2026, explicitly accounting for the fact that P&I war-risk cover was commercially withdrawn as early as 5 March 2026 and that the 25 May 2026 CENTCOM strikes on Iranian missile sites and mine-laying boats constitute new kinetic activity that resets underwriter loss assumptions for the region. The review must identify each asset or counterparty position that is currently uninsured or underinsured for war risk given those reset assumptions, obtain indicative re-quotation terms from at least two war-risk underwriters where coverage has lapsed, and quantify the uninsured loss exposure on a worst-case single-incident basis. The completed review, including the gap analysis and re-quotation terms received, must be submitted to the CFO and Audit Committee Chair by 30 May 2026, with a board-level disclosure recommendation if uninsured exposure exceeds a materiality threshold defined by Legal and Finance jointly.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic insurance-adequacy requirement does not treat the 25 May 2026 strikes as an actuarial reset event separate from the original commercial withdrawal in March 2026; this adaptation requires the review to address both the pre-existing coverage gap from March and the incremental underwriting-assumption shift caused by the new strike activity.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Prepare a stakeholder Q&A document specifically addressing the
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Head of Investor Relations, in coordination with the General Counsel and Chief Communications Officer, must produce a stakeholder Q&A document by 28 May 2026 that prepares the organisation to respond to media and investor inquiries specifically triggered by the 25 May 2026 CENTCOM announcement of self-defense strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and mine-emplacement boats in the Strait of Hormuz. The document must address at minimum: (i) the organisation's direct dependency on Strait of Hormuz transit for its operations or supply chain; (ii) the organisation's exposure to the US secondary-sanctions risk arising from the Persian Gulf Strait Authority's transit-fee regime established 5 May 2026; (iii) the organisation's financial sensitivity to continued Strait closure given the blockade in effect since 28 February 2026; and (iv) the organisation's position on the contested ceasefire status in light of Iran's condemnation of the strikes. Each Q&A entry must include an approved answer, the name of the spokesperson authorised to deliver it, and any legal or disclosure caveats. The finalised document must be approved by the General Counsel before distribution to communications and IR staff.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic stakeholder Q&A requirement does not identify the specific inquiry vectors — secondary-sanctions exposure via the Persian Gulf Strait Authority, contested ceasefire status, and the February 2026 blockade baseline — that are uniquely raised by this incident and that journalists and investors are most likely to probe; this adaptation pre-populates the Q&A framework with those named exposure dimensions.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Coordinate with investor-relations on disclosure language distinguishing direct
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: The Head of Investor Relations must work with the CFO and General Counsel to develop and lock consistent disclosure language by 30 May 2026 that clearly distinguishes the organisation's direct Strait of Hormuz transit exposure — vessels, cargo, or counterparties physically transiting the waterway blocked since 28 February 2026 — from its indirect energy-input cost exposure driven by the global price effects of the Strait closure and the 25 May 2026 CENTCOM escalation, ensuring this distinction is applied uniformly across all earnings call scripts, prepared remarks, and any 8-K or equivalent regulatory filing triggered by the 25 May event. The language must be internally consistent in both its quantitative framing (using the FP&A sensitivity analysis produced under the linked FIN-024 control as its numerical source) and its qualitative characterisation of the organisation's risk position, and must not conflate the two exposure types in a manner that could mislead investors about the nature or magnitude of either. The finalised language templates must be reviewed and signed off by the General Counsel and Chief Disclosure Officer before use in any external communication, with version-controlled copies retained as evidence of completion.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic disclosure-consistency requirement does not specify that the direct/indirect distinction is materially important precisely because the Strait has been physically blocked since February 2026 — meaning direct transit exposure and indirect commodity-price exposure arise from structurally different causal chains — and that conflating them in earnings disclosures could misrepresent the organisation's actual operational risk to investors.

incidents.headline: Victoria's Secret website taken down amid cyberattack, crisis comms failure
incidents.summary: Victoria's Secret detected a 'security incident' on approximately May 24–25, 2026 and took down its U.S. e-commerce website and some in-store services on May 26 — the start of the critical Memorial Day shopping weekend. The company's vague, delayed public messaging ('we identified and are taking steps to address a security incident') with no timeline or data-exposure updates fuelled widespread customer frustration on Instagram and social media, generating significant negative sentiment and trust erosion. The company confirmed in a subsequent June 3 statement that the shutdown ran from May 26 through May 29; the incident was later estimated to cost approximately $20 million, and cybersecurity analysts linked the attack pattern to the Scattered Spider (UNC3944) group that had previously targeted UK retailers Marks & Spencer, Co-op, and Harrods.

secondary_mappings.why: The underlying cause of the reputational harm was a cyberattack — suspected Scattered Spider/DragonForce ransomware intrusion — that forced the e-commerce site offline.
The website and some in-store services were shut down for four days over the Memorial Day peak trading weekend, constituting a material operational continuity failure.
The nature of the security incident raised potential customer data exposure concerns, triggering possible notification obligations and consumer privacy risk.
A potential data breach of a major US retailer triggers state breach-notification laws and possible SEC cyber-disclosure obligations, implicating regulatory reporting controls.

adaptive_objectives.statement: Ensure that crisis communications during a material security incident are timely, accurate, and sufficiently detailed to prevent trust erosion and regulatory scrutiny.
Detect and contain social-engineering-enabled intrusions — consistent with Scattered Spider's SIM-swap and help-desk ruse TTPs — before lateral movement reaches e-commerce and corporate IT systems.
Maintain e-commerce and in-store service continuity, or execute a pre-tested failover, during a ransomware-driven system shutdown affecting peak trading periods such as Memorial Day weekend.
Ensure that corporate systems required for financial reporting can be isolated and recovered within a window that does not force postponement of SEC-mandated quarterly earnings disclosures.
Protect customer PII and loyalty-programme data held in corporate systems from unauthorised access during a ransomware or extortion-driven breach event.
Meet SEC cybersecurity incident disclosure obligations and state breach-notification timelines in a manner that satisfies the materiality and timeliness standards of the December 2023 SEC rules.
Rebuild customer trust and brand equity eroded by the Memorial Day website outage and vague public messaging through structured post-incident communication and demonstrated remediation evidence.

graded_claims.claim: Victoria's Secret detected unauthorized access to its IT systems on approximately May 24, 2026 and took its U.S. e-commerce website offline.
Victoria's Secret filed an SEC disclosure confirming the website shutdown ran from May 26 through May 29 and that corporate systems remained impaired beyond that date.
Victoria's Secret postponed its fiscal Q1 2026 earnings release because employees could not access systems required to produce the financial information.
CFO Scott Sekella disclosed the incident is expected to cost approximately $20 million in lost net sales impact in Q2 fiscal 2025.
Victoria's Secret stock fell approximately 6-7% on the day the breach news broke, with shares closing at around $20.99.
The attack pattern has been linked by cybersecurity analysts to Scattered Spider (UNC3944), the same group suspected in the Marks & Spencer, Co-op, and Harrods attacks.
Victoria's Secret has not officially confirmed Scattered Spider as the attacker; the attribution is based on TTPs and analyst assessment, not company disclosure.
The DragonForce ransomware operation was reportedly used in the UK retail attacks and may be linked to the Victoria's Secret incident, though not confirmed for this specific incident.
Some employees were locked out of email accounts, and a CEO note from Hillary Super warned employees 'recovery is going to take a while.'
Attackers may have accessed customer PII, employee records, third-party vendor information, and internal company data.
A class action lawsuit has been filed in Ohio federal court alleging Victoria's Secret failed to protect customers' personally identifiable information.
Victoria's Secret's vague public messaging ('we identified and are taking steps to address a security incident') generated widespread customer frustration on social media.
The FBI delivered cyber-intelligence briefings to major U.S. retailers in response to the escalating Scattered Spider threat campaign.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Victoria's Secret must issue a structured public update
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Victoria's Secret's Chief Communications Officer must publish a structured public statement no later than June 10, 2026 that explicitly confirms the SEC-disclosed outage window of May 26–29, 2026, identifies which systems (U.S. e-commerce website, in-store services, corporate systems) remained impaired beyond May 29, and states — with specificity — whether customer PII was or was not accessed, replacing the vague 'we identified and are taking steps to address a security incident' language that generated widespread negative sentiment on Instagram and social media. The statement must be disseminated simultaneously across the company's official website, Instagram account, and X (Twitter) handle, and a copy must be attached to any subsequent SEC 8-K amendment filed in connection with the incident. Evidence of completion: timestamped published statement URL, 8-K exhibit reference, and a social-media post archive confirming cross-channel publication before 23:59 ET on June 10, 2026.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a structured disclosure update; this adaptation specifies the exact channels that failed (Instagram), the precise outage dates confirmed in the SEC filing (May 26–29), the specific vague language that must be retired, and the hard deadline of June 10, 2026 — anchoring accountability to the actual public-trust failure rather than a hypothetical disclosure gap.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: A dedicated crisis-communications protocol for ransomware events must
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Victoria's Secret's Head of Digital Communications must, within 30 days of June 10, 2026, develop and obtain board-level pre-approval for a ransomware-specific crisis-communications protocol that mandates a first public social-media post on Instagram and X within 4 hours of any unplanned e-commerce site takedown, with a prescribed message template that acknowledges the outage, names affected customer channels (specifically PINK Nation loyalty-order fulfilment), and commits to a named update cadence of no less than every 6 hours until service is restored. The protocol must include pre-drafted holding statements calibrated to the four outage-severity tiers, designated posting authority for each tier, and an Instagram-specific rapid-response escalation path that bypasses standard content-approval workflows during declared cybersecurity incidents. Evidence of completion: board-ratified protocol document, signed approval record, and at least one tabletop test log demonstrating the 4-hour social-media posting obligation was met.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control calls for a pre-approved crisis-comms protocol; this adaptation names the specific channels that failed during the May 26 Memorial Day outage (Instagram, PINK Nation order fulfilment), sets the 4-hour social-media posting window against the actual response delay, and prescribes Instagram-specific bypass procedures to address the documented approval-lag failure mode.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Victoria's Secret must appoint a named external spokesperson
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Within 10 business days of June 10, 2026, Victoria's Secret's CEO Hillary Super must formally designate — and publicly name — an external cybersecurity communications specialist (e.g., a recognized incident-response communications firm or a credentialed CISO-level advisor) to serve as the company's sole authoritative external spokesperson for all public statements related to the May 2026 cyberattack, with authority to issue verified technical briefings to media, cybersecurity analysts, and investor audiences independent of CEO internal memoranda. This spokesperson must be introduced in the June 10 public update referenced in AC-REP-003-RNS-01, must have demonstrable cybersecurity incident-response credentials (e.g., prior named involvement in a publicly disclosed retail-sector breach response), and must remain in the role until the forensic investigation closes and a final public report is issued. Evidence of completion: named spokesperson bio published on Victoria's Secret's newsroom page, spokesperson credit line in the June 10 public statement, and a signed engagement letter retained by the General Counsel.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires an external spokesperson with cybersecurity credentials; this adaptation distinguishes the external spokesperson role from the internal CEO memo channel used during the May 2026 incident (specifically Hillary Super's reported staff communications), anchors the appointment to the confirmed June 10 public-update milestone, and sets a credential standard derived from the retail-sector attack pattern context.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Implement phishing-resistant MFA on all Victoria's Secret help-desk
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Victoria's Secret's CISO must, within 21 calendar days of June 10, 2026, mandate phishing-resistant MFA (FIDO2/WebAuthn hardware tokens or equivalent) across all IT help-desk identity-reset and account-recovery workflows — including password resets, MFA seed reissuance, and any exception-override pathways — specifically to eliminate the SIM-swap and social-engineering help-desk ruse entry vectors documented in Scattered Spider (UNC3944) campaigns against UK retailers Marks & Spencer, Co-op, and Harrods in the same attack cycle. No identity-reset action may be completed by help-desk staff without a verified phishing-resistant authenticator challenge; voice-based or SMS-based verification for account recovery must be disabled for all corporate accounts by the 21-day deadline. Evidence of completion: updated help-desk identity-reset procedure document, configuration screenshots from the identity provider showing SMS/voice fallback disabled, and a privileged-access review confirming zero active SMS-based MFA seeds on help-desk-accessible accounts.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires phishing-resistant MFA on help-desk workflows; this adaptation ties the control directly to the Scattered Spider SIM-swap and help-desk ruse TTPs linked by analysts to the Victoria's Secret May 2026 breach, sets a 21-day hard deadline, and explicitly requires disabling SMS/voice fallback — the specific authentication weakness exploited in the peer retail-sector attacks.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Deploy micro-segmentation controls to isolate Victoria's Secret's e-commerce
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Victoria's Secret's CISO and CTO must, within 60 calendar days of June 10, 2026, implement network micro-segmentation controls that enforce a hard isolation boundary between the U.S. e-commerce platform and the corporate ERP environment (including the financial-reporting and earnings-production systems that were rendered inaccessible during the May 26–29 outage), such that a compromise of one network zone cannot propagate laterally to the other without traversing an authenticated, logged, and anomaly-monitored control point. Segmentation must cover all data paths — including API integrations, shared service accounts, and backup replication channels — that currently bridge the e-commerce and corporate ERP domains, and must be validated by a purple-team lateral-movement exercise conducted no later than 75 days after June 10. Evidence of completion: approved network segmentation architecture diagram signed by CISO, purple-team exercise report confirming lateral movement from e-commerce to ERP is blocked, and a remediation closure memo filed with the Audit Committee.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control calls for micro-segmentation to prevent lateral movement; this adaptation anchors the requirement to the specific confirmed failure — the simultaneous takedown of both the U.S. e-commerce website and the earnings-production systems during the May 26–29 outage — names the precise system boundary that must be enforced, and mandates a purple-team validation to close the gap that enabled the SEC-reportable earnings postponement.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Establish a Scattered Spider-specific threat-intelligence feed (aligned with
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Victoria's Secret's SOC Manager must, within 14 calendar days of June 10, 2026, onboard a Scattered Spider (UNC3944) and DragonForce ransomware-specific threat-intelligence feed — sourced from or validated against Mandiant's May 2026 UNC3944 retail-sector advisory and FBI cyber-intelligence briefings delivered to major U.S. retailers — and translate that feed into active SIEM detection rules covering DragonForce ransomware beacon signatures, Cobalt Strike C2 indicators, and Scattered Spider's documented help-desk social-engineering precursor activity. The SOC must conduct a retrospective hunt across Victoria's Secret's network telemetry for the period May 1–25, 2026 using these indicators within 7 days of feed onboarding, and produce a written hunt report delivered to the CISO. Evidence of completion: signed threat-intelligence subscription or feed-ingestion record, SIEM rule-set deployment log with indicator coverage mapped to UNC3944 TTPs, and the completed retrospective hunt report with findings and any escalations documented.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a threat-intelligence feed integrated into SOC detection rules; this adaptation specifies the exact adversary (Scattered Spider/UNC3944), the specific malware family (DragonForce ransomware), the authoritative source (Mandiant's May 2026 retail-sector warning and FBI briefings confirmed in reporting), and mandates a retroactive network hunt covering the pre-breach window — actions the generic control does not prescribe.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Activate a pre-tested e-commerce failover site capable of
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Victoria's Secret's VP of E-commerce Operations must, within 45 calendar days of June 10, 2026, activate and fully test a pre-provisioned e-commerce failover environment — architecturally isolated from the primary corporate IT domain — capable of accepting and fulfilling PINK Nation loyalty-programme orders within 4 hours of a corporate-network shutdown declaration, specifically designed to prevent a recurrence of the four-day (May 26–29) Memorial Day weekend revenue loss confirmed in the SEC filing and estimated at approximately $20 million in net sales impact. The failover environment must support at minimum: product browsing, checkout, PINK Nation loyalty-points redemption, and order-status tracking; it must be tested under simulated corporate-network isolation conditions no later than the 45-day deadline, and must be certified ready for activation before the Q4 2026 holiday shopping season. Evidence of completion: failover test plan, load-test results demonstrating 4-hour activation capability, signed readiness certification from the VP of E-commerce, and a briefing to the Board Risk Committee.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires an e-commerce failover site; this adaptation specifies the PINK Nation loyalty-programme as the critical order channel that was disrupted, ties the readiness benchmark to the confirmed four-day Memorial Day outage and the reported $20 million net sales impact, and sets a Q4 holiday season deadline to ensure the control is operative before the next peak revenue period.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Victoria's Secret must pre-stage a financial-reporting continuity environment
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Victoria's Secret's CFO Scott Sekella and CTO must jointly, within 45 calendar days of June 10, 2026, establish and validate a financial-reporting continuity environment — hosted on infrastructure with no shared authentication, network, or replication path to the primary corporate IT domain — capable of producing SEC-required earnings materials (including Q1 fiscal 2026 revenue, earnings, and segment data) independently of corporate ERP system availability, specifically to prevent a recurrence of the SEC-reportable postponement of the Q1 2026 earnings release caused by employees' inability to access financial-reporting systems during the May 26–29 outage. The environment must be tested with a full dry-run production of Q1 fiscal 2026 financials no later than the 45-day deadline, and the CFO must certify in writing to the Audit Committee that earnings publication timelines can be met even during a sustained corporate IT outage of up to 7 days. Evidence of completion: architecture diagram confirming domain isolation, dry-run test results and discrepancy resolution log, and CFO's signed certification letter to the Audit Committee.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a financial-reporting continuity environment; this adaptation names the specific SEC-reportable failure (postponed Q1 2026 earnings release), names CFO Scott Sekella as accountable owner, sets a 7-day outage tolerance benchmark derived from the confirmed May 26–29 shutdown duration and reported ongoing system impairment, and ties the dry-run deadline to a concrete fiscal quarter.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Conduct a full recovery-time objective test for Victoria's
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Victoria's Secret's CISO and VP of IT Operations must, within 60 calendar days of June 10, 2026, conduct a formal recovery-time objective (RTO) test for both the U.S. e-commerce platform and the corporate ERP/financial-reporting systems, benchmarked against the confirmed four-day (May 26–May 29) outage duration and the extended post-May-29 corporate-system impairment disclosed in the SEC filing, with a certification target of full e-commerce restoration within 24 hours and financial-reporting system restoration within 48 hours for any future peak-period incident. The test must simulate an adversarial shutdown consistent with the May 2026 incident scope — including simultaneous e-commerce and ERP unavailability — and must be conducted in a non-production environment that mirrors current production architecture post any segmentation changes made under AC-CYB-026-RNS-01. Evidence of completion: RTO test plan, timestamped test execution log, independent third-party attestation of results, and a gap remediation plan for any system that fails to meet the 24/48-hour targets, filed with the Board Risk Committee.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires an RTO test benchmarked against the actual outage; this adaptation specifies the exact confirmed outage duration (May 26–29) and extended impairment as the benchmark, distinguishes 24-hour and 48-hour targets across the two system classes, and requires the test architecture to reflect post-segmentation changes — ensuring the control is not invalidated by concurrent remediation work.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Immediately audit access logs for Victoria's Secret's loyalty-programme
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Victoria's Secret's CISO must, within 10 business days of June 10, 2026, commission a forensic audit of all access logs, query logs, and data-movement records associated with the PINK Nation loyalty-programme database covering the window of May 22–26, 2026 — the period beginning approximately two days before detected unauthorized access and ending at the confirmed May 26 site shutdown — to determine whether any exfiltration of PINK Nation member PII (including names, email addresses, purchase history, and payment-card data) occurred prior to shutdown, as alleged in the Ohio federal class action filed against Victoria's Secret. The forensic audit must be conducted by a qualified independent third-party forensics firm, must produce a written findings report within 30 days of commission, and must be shared in privileged form with outside litigation counsel managing the Ohio class action and with the General Counsel; if exfiltration is confirmed or cannot be ruled out, the findings must immediately trigger the notification workflow under AC-DAT-012-RNS-01. Evidence of completion: signed forensics engagement letter, chain-of-custody log for evidence collected, and final forensic findings report delivered to General Counsel and litigation counsel within the 30-day window.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires an access-log audit for the loyalty-programme database; this adaptation specifies the exact May 22–26 audit window derived from the reported detection timeline, names PINK Nation as the specific database, ties the findings directly to the pending Ohio federal class action, and creates an explicit trigger-link to the breach-notification control — ensuring the audit result has a defined downstream action rather than being an isolated forensic exercise.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Issue state-law breach notifications to affected customers in
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Victoria's Secret's General Counsel and Chief Privacy Officer must, no later than 5 business days after the forensic findings report required under AC-DAT-005-RNS-01 is delivered (or immediately if PII exposure is confirmed before that report is complete), initiate state-law breach notifications to all potentially affected customers in Ohio — in compliance with Ohio's 45-day notification deadline under the Ohio Data Protection Act and the Ohio Revised Code § 1349.19 — and in all other U.S. jurisdictions with applicable mandatory notification windows, with notification letters that identify the nature of the exposed PII, the May 22–26, 2026 exposure window, the steps Victoria's Secret is taking to remediate, and credit-monitoring or identity-protection offers where required by law. A notification tracker documenting each state, the applicable statutory deadline, the dispatch date, and the delivery confirmation must be maintained and produced to the Ohio Attorney General's office as required under coordination obligations in AC-REG-019-RNS-01. Evidence of completion: state-by-state notification tracker signed by the Chief Privacy Officer, sample notification letter reviewed by outside privacy counsel, and proof of dispatch records for Ohio and all other triggered jurisdictions.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires state-law breach notifications; this adaptation identifies Ohio as the priority jurisdiction anchored to the confirmed Ohio federal class-action filing, specifies the Ohio statutory framework and 45-day deadline, links the notification trigger to the forensic findings from AC-DAT-005-RNS-01 rather than leaving the trigger undefined, and mandates a state-by-state tracker that feeds directly into the regulatory coordination obligation.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Victoria's Secret must ensure all subsequent 8-K amendments
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Victoria's Secret's General Counsel must ensure that every subsequent SEC 8-K amendment filed in connection with the May 2026 cyberattack — beginning with the first amendment filed after June 10, 2026 — satisfies the SEC's December 2023 cybersecurity disclosure rules by including: (a) an explicit, board-reviewed materiality determination referencing the confirmed $20 million estimated net sales impact and the approximately 6–7% single-day stock decline; (b) a disclosure of the current status of the forensic investigation into potential customer PII exfiltration, including whether exfiltration has been confirmed, ruled out, or remains undetermined as of the filing date; and (c) a description of any material changes to Victoria's Secret's cybersecurity risk profile resulting from the incident. Each 8-K amendment must be reviewed by outside securities counsel with SEC cybersecurity disclosure expertise and by the Audit Committee before filing. Evidence of completion: filed 8-K amendment with required disclosures present, outside counsel review memo, and Audit Committee approval minutes for each amendment.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires SEC cybersecurity disclosure rule compliance; this adaptation anchors the materiality determination to the specific confirmed financial metrics (reported $20 million net sales impact, reported 6–7% stock decline) already in the public record, requires each amendment to state the current PII-investigation status as an ongoing disclosure obligation, and mandates Audit Committee sign-off — specificity that the generic control does not require.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Coordinate with the Ohio Attorney General's office on
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Victoria's Secret's General Counsel and Chief Privacy Officer must, within 10 business days of June 10, 2026, initiate direct engagement with the Ohio Attorney General's Cybercrime Section to (a) confirm the applicable notification timeline under Ohio Revised Code § 1349.19 for the May 2026 incident, (b) provide the Attorney General's office with a written summary of Victoria's Secret's breach investigation status and notification timeline plan, and (c) document the agreed notification cadence and any safe-harbor or cure-period determinations in a written communication retained in the incident file. The agreed notification cadence and any Attorney General correspondence must be summarized and disclosed in the next 8-K amendment filed under AC-REG-003-RNS-01, in compliance with the SEC's December 2023 rule requirement to disclose material legal proceedings arising from a cybersecurity incident. Evidence of completion: dated correspondence log with Ohio Attorney General's office, written summary document transmitted to the AG, and reference to the AG engagement in the next 8-K amendment filing.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires coordination with state authorities; this adaptation specifies the Ohio Attorney General as the primary regulatory counterparty triggered by the confirmed Ohio federal class action, names the specific Ohio statute, requires the outcome of that coordination to be fed into the SEC 8-K disclosure chain — closing the loop between state regulatory compliance and federal disclosure obligations.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Establish a post-incident trust-recovery programme for PINK Nation
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Victoria's Secret's Chief Marketing Officer and Chief Communications Officer must, within 30 days of the forensic investigation closing (or no later than October 1, 2026, whichever is earlier), launch a structured PINK Nation trust-recovery programme that includes: (a) a public acknowledgment — published on the Victoria's Secret website, the PINK Nation app, and the company's Instagram account — that the Memorial Day weekend (May 26–29, 2026) outage disrupted PINK Nation members' ability to place and track orders during a peak shopping period; (b) a loyalty-points remediation offer of defined value (to be set by the CMO but no less than a meaningful goodwill gesture as determined in consultation with Legal) credited automatically to all PINK Nation accounts active in the 30 days prior to May 26, 2026; and (c) a transparent forensic-findings summary — reviewed by Legal and the external cybersecurity spokesperson designated under AC-REP-011-RNS-01 — stating what was investigated, what was found regarding customer data, and what remediation steps were taken. Evidence of completion: published public acknowledgment with timestamp, PINK Nation loyalty-points credit confirmation report from the CRM system, and forensic-findings summary published on the Victoria's Secret newsroom page.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a post-incident trust-recovery programme; this adaptation names PINK Nation as the specific loyalty programme disrupted, ties the loyalty-points remediation to the active-account population as of May 26, anchors the forensic-findings summary to the spokesperson designated under AC-REP-011-RNS-01 for credibility, and sets an October 1 outside deadline to ensure the programme launches before the Q4 holiday season.

adaptive_controls_v2.parent_mc_title: Conduct a post-incident lessons-learned review of the May 26
adaptive_controls_v2.statement: Victoria's Secret's Chief Communications Officer must, within 60 calendar days of June 10, 2026, lead a structured post-incident lessons-learned review of the May 26, 2026 Memorial Day crisis-communications failure — specifically examining the decision to use a single vague statement ('we identified and are taking steps to address a security incident'), the absence of a social-media response cadence on Instagram during the four-day outage, the lack of customer-specific messaging for PINK Nation order impacts, and the gap between the May 24 detection date and the first public communication — and must produce a written Crisis Communications Failure Report that is shared with the Board's Risk Committee. Based on that report, the CCO must codify a revised retail-sector cyber-crisis communications standard — incorporating a mandated 4-hour first-post rule, a pre-approved Instagram rapid-response template library, and a PINK Nation-specific customer-impact messaging protocol — and certify its readiness to the CEO and Board no later than September 30, 2026, to ensure the updated standard is operative before the Q4 2026 holiday shopping season. Evidence of completion: Crisis Communications Failure Report delivered to Board Risk Committee, revised communications standard document approved by CEO, and signed readiness certification filed with Board records before September 30, 2026.
adaptive_controls_v2.rationale: The generic master control requires a post-incident lessons-learned review and updated comms standard; this adaptation dissects the four specific failure modes documented in the May 26 incident (vague statement, no cadence, no PINK Nation messaging, delayed first communication), names Instagram as the primary failed channel, and sets a September 30 hard deadline that deliberately precedes the Q4 holiday peak — the same high-stakes period during which the May 26 failure occurred.

incidents.headline: GKN Aerospace Garden Grove MMA tank explosion threat eliminated after mass evacuation
incidents.summary: Beginning May 21, 2026, a 34,000-gallon methyl methacrylate (MMA) storage tank at the GKN Aerospace manufacturing facility in Garden Grove, California began overheating and off-gassing, triggering a multi-day crisis. Mandatory evacuation orders affecting over 50,000 residents across a nine-square-mile zone were issued as authorities warned of a potential Boiling Liquid Expanding Vapor Explosion (BLEVE); damaged tank valves prevented chemical neutralisation or drainage. On May 25, 2026 — squarely within the reporting window — Orange County Fire Authority officials announced the catastrophic explosion threat was 'now off the table' after a crack formed enabling pressure release, though a reduced 16,000-person evacuation zone remained in force.

secondary_mappings.why: A five-day multi-agency hazmat emergency involving California state-of-emergency declaration, 785 first responders, EPA deployment, and mandatory mass evacuations constitutes a full operational crisis.
Potential uncontrolled release of thousands of gallons of toxic, flammable MMA into the surrounding area was the primary environmental risk driving the emergency response.

GKN Aerospace has prior OSHA and California DIR violations at this same facility, and a class-action lawsuit was filed on May 23 seeking hundreds of millions in damages; regulatory scrutiny of facility safety records is active.
Mass media coverage, California governor state-of-emergency declaration, and class-action filing generate significant reputational exposure for GKN Aerospace.
50,000+ residents and facility workers were exposed to displacement risk and potential toxic chemical harm; school closures and evacuation centres were activated.

incidents.severity_rationale: Mass civilian evacuation (50,000+ people), California state of emergency, credible catastrophic explosion risk at an aerospace facility, and active class-action litigation warrant high severity; capped at 4 as no explosion occurred and no injuries were reported.
incidents.financial_impact_disclosed: Class-action lawsuit seeking 'hundreds of millions or billions' of dollars filed 2026-05-23; no corporate financial figure formally disclosed.

adaptive_objectives.statement: Ensure physical infrastructure containing hazardous process chemicals is maintained with operable pressure-relief and drainage systems capable of safe neutralisation or emergency discharge.

Maintain operational continuity plans that account for multi-day facility shutdown triggered by a chemical process safety emergency, including supply-chain notification and alternative production routing.
Control environmental release of listed hazardous substances — including MMA under EPCRA Section 302 extremely hazardous substance thresholds — to prevent offsite community exposure.

Maintain compliance with OSHA PSM and EPA RMP requirements applicable to facilities storing flammable reactive chemicals above threshold quantities, and cooperate fully with post-incident regulatory investigations.
Manage public and stakeholder communications during a multi-day hazmat emergency in a way that protects organisational reputation and supports community trust in the resolution process.
Protect the health and safety of facility workers and the surrounding community from acute hazardous chemical exposure events, including provision for shelter-in-place and evacuation coordination.
